ucs.title: [LLM] Aquatic Panda Log4Shell exploitation of VMware Horizon Tomcat spawning recon LOLBins
ucs.description: Hunts the exact post-exploitation chain CrowdStrike OverWatch attributed to Aquatic Panda (G0143) in December 2021: Log4Shell against VMware Horizon causes the Apache Tomcat service (ws_TomcatService.exe / java.exe under the Horizon Server path) to spawn recon LOLBins (whoami, hostname, ping, net, systeminfo, tasklist) and PowerShell/cmd, followed by WMIC and rundll32. Java/Tomcat is not a normal parent for these binaries — when it is, the actor's hands-on-keyboard footprint is unmistakable.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("ws_TomcatService.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat8.exe","tomcat7.exe") OR (Processes.parent_process_name="java.exe" AND (Processes.parent_process_path="*\\VMware\\VMware View\\Server\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\VMware\\Horizon\\*" OR Processes.parent_process_path="*\\Tomcat\\*"))) AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ping.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let HorizonParents = dynamic(["ws_tomcatservice.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat8.exe","tomcat7.exe"]);
let HorizonPaths = dynamic([@"\VMware\VMware View\Server\", @"\VMware\Horizon\", @"\Tomcat\"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ping.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (HorizonParents)
    or (InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "java.exe" and InitiatingProcessFolderPath has_any (HorizonPaths))
| where FileName in~ (SuspiciousChildren)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentPath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildBinary  = FileName,
          ChildCmd     = ProcessCommandLine,
          SHA256, IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let HorizonParents = dynamic(["ws_tomcatservice.exe","tomcat9.exe","tomcat8.exe","tomcat7.exe"]);
let HorizonPaths = dynamic([@"\VMware\VMware View\Server\", @"\VMware\Horizon\", @"\Tomcat\"]);
let SuspiciousChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmic.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","ping.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend ParentLeaf = tolower(tostring(split(ParentProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| extend ChildLeaf  = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName,    "\\")[-1]))
| where ParentLeaf in (HorizonParents)
    or (ParentLeaf == "java.exe" and ParentProcessName has_any (HorizonPaths))
| where ChildLeaf in (SuspiciousChildren)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Aquatic Panda Log4Shell Post-Exploitation - Tomcat/Horizon spawning Recon LOLBins
id: 4f9a2c1d-3b56-4f4a-8e5e-aquatic-panda-log4shell
status: experimental
description: Detects the post-exploitation hands-on-keyboard activity attributed to Aquatic Panda (G0143) following Log4Shell exploitation of VMware Horizon, where ws_TomcatService.exe / java.exe spawns recon LOLBins, cmd.exe and PowerShell.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0143/
  - https://www.crowdstrike.com/blog/overwatch-exposes-aquatic-panda-in-possession-of-log-4-shell-exploit-tools/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.t1082
  - attack.g0143
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  parent_tomcat:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\ws_TomcatService.exe'
      - '\tomcat9.exe'
      - '\tomcat8.exe'
      - '\tomcat7.exe'
  parent_horizon_java:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\java.exe'
    ParentImage|contains:
      - '\VMware\VMware View\Server\'
      - '\VMware\Horizon\'
      - '\Tomcat\'
  child_lolbins:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wmic.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\tasklist.exe'
      - '\ping.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
  condition: (parent_tomcat or parent_horizon_java) and child_lolbins
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate Horizon admin scripts; whitelist by host or by parent command line.
level: high
ucs.rationale: Java/Tomcat under a Horizon Server path is virtually never a legitimate parent of cmd.exe, powershell.exe, whoami, net, or rundll32. CrowdStrike OverWatch's December 2021 publication 'OverWatch Exposes AQUATIC PANDA in Possession of Log4Shell Exploit Tools' described this exact chain (ws_TomcatService.exe → whoami / hostname / ping / netstat / cmd) as the actor's hands-on-keyboard fingerprint. Pin

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Aquatic Panda LSASS MiniDump via rundll32 + comsvcs.dll
ucs.description: Hunts the credential-access primitive Aquatic Panda used after Horizon/Tomcat compromise: invoking the MiniDump export of comsvcs.dll through rundll32 to dump LSASS without dropping a custom binary. CrowdStrike's reporting documented this as one of the actor's preferred living-off-the-land credential-theft methods.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" AND Processes.process="*comsvcs*" AND (Processes.process="*MiniDump*" OR Processes.process="*minidump*" OR Processes.process="*#24*" OR Processes.process="*#-24*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.process_guid | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename host as dest | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("MiniDump", "minidump", "#24", "#-24")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentBinary = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd    = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentPath   = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession,
          SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where NewProcessName endswith "\\rundll32.exe"
| where CommandLine has "comsvcs"
| where CommandLine has_any ("MiniDump", "minidump", "#24", "#-24")
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, SubjectUserName,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: LSASS MiniDump via comsvcs.dll (Aquatic Panda TTP)
id: 8e1d7f3b-2a4c-4a0e-9cc1-aquatic-panda-comsvcs
status: stable
description: Detects rundll32.exe invoking the MiniDump export of comsvcs.dll, an LSASS credential-dumping primitive used by Aquatic Panda (G0143).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0143/
  - https://www.crowdstrike.com/blog/overwatch-exposes-aquatic-panda-in-possession-of-log-4-shell-exploit-tools/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.t1218.011
  - attack.g0143
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_img:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
  selection_cli_dll:
    CommandLine|contains: 'comsvcs'
  selection_cli_export:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'MiniDump'
      - 'minidump'
      - '#24'
      - '#-24'
  condition: selection_img and selection_cli_dll and selection_cli_export
falsepositives:
  - Extremely rare; legitimate diagnostic dumps typically use procdump/Task Manager, not comsvcs.
level: critical
ucs.rationale: rundll32.exe loading comsvcs.dll's MiniDump export against lsass.exe is not a benign administrative pattern — Microsoft does not ship tooling that uses this code path for diagnostics. CrowdStrike's Aquatic Panda intrusion report specifically documented this LOLBin combination as the actor's chosen LSASS dump method (alongside connection attempts to obscure the SHA from defenders). Pairing it with

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
